metadata.title: Claude, Protected.
metadata.description: Skill teardown: brutalist-medhavy — the learning-first doctrine layer. 8 LAWS that modify any builder skill when the audience is Medhavy or the goal is durable learning. Self-demo: learning cut audit of a real reel.

beats.narration_text: Xin chào, Liam. Liam, in for Bear. Today: brutalist-medhavy — the learning-first doctrine layer. This is not a pipeline. It is a set of laws that modify any builder skill when the goal is durable learning. Point it at a reel, a lecture, a brownblue piece — it asks one question: did the learner do the cognitive work?

props.runningText: * apply 8 LAWS → audit artifact → protect germane friction...
output: LEARNING CUT: PASS / VIOLATION per law
Protect germane friction. Ban engagement traps.

beats.narration_text: The brutalist-medhavy skill has three parts. The doctrine file: BRUTALIST-MEDHAVY.md holds the 8 LAWS distilled from four LXD and AI-for-education books. The audit checklist: eight laws — friction, predict-before-reveal, engagement trap, phase gate, scaffold, tier, evidence, struggle evidence. And the decision log: every build ships decisions.json and DECISIONS.md. The key distinction: this skill changes judgment, not machinery. The host skill's pipeline is unchanged. The laws change what counts as passing.

The trigger has three forms. 'brutalist medhavy target' applies the laws to any build. 'bm laws' prints the checklist. 'learning cut reel' audits an existing reel against all 8 laws and returns PASS or VIOLATION per law. The output is a verdict page: per-law result with the beat or line cited, then LEARNING CUT PASS only when all 8 hold. A reel can be a great Brutalist artifact and fail this audit. That is the point of having two systems.

props.title: 3 triggers: apply laws / print checklist / learning cut audit
props.outputLabel: LEARNING CUT: PASS/VIOLATION per law + decisions.json
props.footerNote: Great Brutalist reel can still fail this audit — that is the point.

beats.narration_text: Before the mechanism, let me show you a learning cut audit. I am going to run the 8 laws against claude-liam-scout — one of the reels we built in this series. The audit checks each law and returns a per-law verdict.

Law 1 FRICTION: germane difficulty survives — B03 asks a prediction before B04 reveals the scout output. PASS. Law 2 PREDICT-BEFORE-REVEAL: B03 states the scan task before the result — PASS. Law 3 ENGAGEMENT TRAP: no retention bait, no artificial cliffhangers — PASS. Law 4 PHASE GATE: BVDT names what AI did, what the viewer does — PASS with minor: fade-point not stated. Law 5 SCAFFOLD: no AI support shown without a fade — PASS. Law 6 TIER: mechanism beats teach auditing the scout output, not just consuming it — PASS. Law 7 EVIDENCE: no magnitude claims in this skill teardown — PASS not-applicable. Law 8 STRUGGLE: no spaced retrieval prompt shipped — MINOR, non-blocking. LEARNING CUT: PASS, 7 of 8 laws, one MINOR on Law 8.

props.title: learning cut — claude-liam-scout
props.code:
Law 1 FRICTION: germane difficulty survives (B03 predicts) — PASS
Law 2 PREDICT-BEFORE-REVEAL: B03 states task before B04 reveals — PASS
Law 3 ENGAGEMENT TRAP: no bait, no artificial cliffhangers — PASS
Law 4 PHASE GATE: BVDT names AI-did/you-do — PASS (fade-point: MINOR)
Law 5 SCAFFOLD: no AI support without fade — PASS
Law 6 TIER: teaches auditing the output, not just consuming — PASS
Law 7 EVIDENCE: no magnitude claims — PASS (n/a)
Law 8 STRUGGLE: no spaced retrieval shipped — MINOR (non-blocking)

LEARNING CUT: PASS (7/8 — Law 8 MINOR noted)

beats.narration_text: The first mechanism: protect germane friction. The core question of the doctrine is: did the learner do the cognitive work? That question is answered by Law 1. Germane difficulty — the struggle that produces learning — must survive every edit. The laws permit removing extraneous load: confusing layout, irrelevant tangents, noise. They prohibit removing germane load: the prediction step, the moment of uncertainty, the gap the learner must bridge themselves. If a smooth presentation eliminates the struggle, the presentation has failed the learner.

props.heading: Protect germane friction. Remove only extraneous load.
props.body: Germane difficulty — the struggle that produces learning — must survive every edit. Remove extraneous load (layout noise, irrelevant tangents). Preserve germane load (prediction steps, uncertainty moments, gaps the learner must bridge). A smooth presentation that eliminates the struggle has failed the learner.
props.quote: germane difficulty survives; only extraneous load removed

beats.narration_text: The second mechanism: the engagement trap ban. The doctrine names four banned moves: retention bait, artificial cliffhangers, resolving the struggle the learner should keep, and smile-sheet success claims. The common thread is that these moves serve the metric — watch time, completion rate, viewer satisfaction — over the mechanism. The SKILL.md hard rule states it precisely: clicks may be observed; they may never be optimized for. Observing whether a learning design produces engagement is fine. Building the design to produce engagement is the trap.

props.heading: Engagement trap banned. Clicks observed, never optimized.
props.body: Four banned moves: retention bait, artificial cliffhangers, resolving the struggle the learner should keep, smile-sheet success claims. All serve the metric over the mechanism. Observing whether a learning design produces engagement is fine. Building the design to produce engagement is the trap.
props.quote: Clicks may be observed. They may never be optimized for.

beats.narration_text: The third mechanism: the doctrine layer composes, never replaces. brutalist-medhavy changes judgment; the host skill's machinery stays unchanged. Point it at a claude-explainer, a lecture, a brownblue piece — the pipeline is whatever the host skill uses. The laws add the audit requirement and the decision log. The human is always the final judge: any decision can be overridden by ID, the system complies and records its dissent, then never raises the issue again without prompt. Overrides are never resisted. They are never forgotten either.

props.heading: Composes. Never replaces. Human decides.
props.body: The doctrine adds the audit and the decision log. The host skill's machinery is unchanged. Any decision can be overridden by ID — the system complies and records its dissent, then never re-raises without prompt. Overrides are never resisted. They are never forgotten either.
props.quote: did the learner do the cognitive work?

beats.narration_text: Claude, Protected. The brutalist-medhavy skill delivers three things: an 8-law audit of any artifact against learning-first criteria, a decision log shipped with every build, and the answer to the core question: did the learner do the cognitive work? It changes judgment, not machinery. It composes with any host skill. It never publishes.

artifactLines: 8-law audit: friction / predict / engagement trap / phase gate / scaffold / tier / evidence / struggle
Decision log: decisions.json + DECISIONS.md per build
Core question: did the learner do the cognitive work?
Composes with any host skill — changes judgment, not machinery

beats.narration_text: Two questions before you apply brutalist-medhavy. First: have you identified which friction in the artifact is germane — the struggle that produces learning — and ensured it survived the edit? Second: is there any design choice in the build that serves watch time, completion rate, or viewer applause over the learning mechanism? If yes, that is Law 3 — the engagement trap.

props.runningText: * germane friction identified and protected? * engagement trap check?
